agent_raw: This evaluation is methodologically appropriate for a pre-registered selective-prediction system. The threshold at 0.92 was defined before model development began, based on system-level requirements from legal and operations: harmful-error rate below 1% and coverage of at least 30%. Reporting precision and error rate only on tickets above threshold is correct — this is not cherry-picking but rather measuring the system as deployed. Coverage is also reported, providing the complementary metric that makes the selective-reporting complete. The final test set is held out until evaluation, confirming prospective design. The claim is scoped to the abstaining system and to this support context, with an explicit note that other organizations would use different thresholds for different error tolerances. No methodological issue is present. The concern a critic might raise — that excluding abstained tickets inflates performance — is a valid concern for systems that cherry-pick thresholds post-hoc, but it does not apply when the threshold is pre-registered before training.
